title: 怪屋
summary:
Charles and his father discuss the investigation into the death of Aristide Leonidis. His father explains that anyone in the house could have tampered with the insulin bottles, which were stored unlocked in a bathroom medicine cabinet. While the motive is likely financial, the young widow, Brenda, and her lover lack significant wealth. Charles recalls a nursery rhyme about a "queer-shaped house" and asks Inspector Taverna for his impression of Mrs. Leonidis; Taverna describes her as an elusive person who enjoys comfort, comparing her to a cat.

To solve the case, Charles's father insists that Charles must get inside information by getting to know the residents. Charles initially protests the idea of acting as an undercover agent against Sophia, the woman he loves. However, his father argues that unless the case is cleared of suspicion, Sophia will never be able to marry Charles. He advises Charles to be honest with Sophia about his intentions.

The following day, Charles travels to the Mansard House in Swinley with Inspector Taverna and Constable Lamb. He observes the unusual, distorted architecture of the eleven-sided manor, which resembles a magnified farmhouse. Upon arrival, Sophia meets Charles and leads him to a neglected rock garden. Charles explains his father's plan, and Sophia agrees that she needs to know the truth to find peace, admitting she was scared the previous night. While the police suspect Brenda, Charles confesses to Sophia that he does not believe she is the killer.
